query: Anthropic interpretability research global workspace Claude internal thoughts findings
a_issues: Content A's core facts (J-space, ~10% of activity, few dozen concepts) come mainly from a Reddit summary rather than primary sources, and its Anthropic.com excerpt is more fragmentary (talks about injecting 'lightning' pattern) without explaining the Jacobian lens technique itself or its theoretical grounding.
b_issues: The LinkedIn excerpt in B includes an out-of-context, confusing line ('Claude and other models do not have private thoughts') that superficially seems to contradict the paper's premise, though this is likely a stripped context/rebuttal rather than a genuine factual error in the underlying research.
reasoning: Both sets correctly identify the core finding (a 'J-space'/global workspace inside Claude found via the Jacobian lens/J-lens). However, Content B gives more substantive and accurate technical detail directly from Anthropic's own page and Indian Express: it names the specific technique (Jacobian lens), explains how it maps activations to vocabulary words, describes a concrete example (the 'France' concept enabling recall of capital/currency/continent), introduces the follow-up technique 'counterfactual reflection training,' and correctly credits Dehaene, Naccache, and Changeux as originators of the global neuronal workspace theory that inspired the work. Content A supplies useful quantitative color (10% of activity, dozens of concepts) via Reddit, but relies more on secondary summarization and provides less mechanistic detail on how the interpretability technique works. Overall, B offers a slightly more complete and technically grounded basis for answering the question about the research's findings and methodology.
